title: ranunculus marginatus var. scandicinus
intro: Ranunculus marginatus var. scandicinus, also known as the Alpine Buttercup, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, the roots and rhizomes of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, particularly in folk remedies, due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits associated with Ranunculus marginatus var. scandicinus include its potential to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
